item.title: CPU Virtualization II: Scheduling
item.description: Introduction In this post, we complete our exploration of cpu virtualization by looking at how the OS and the processor work together to allow user programs to effectively and fairly share the CPU. As mentioned in the previous article, this post is derived from the notes I made while reading the best OS book on the internet CPU Time-Sharing Whenever a program is being executed, the OS is not running.

item.title: CPU Virtualization I: Isolation & Control
item.description: Introduction In this post, we introduce resource virtualization and explore how the operating system uses this idea to give programs fair and efficient access to one of computing’s main resource: the CPU. In future posts, we’ll examine how virtualization works with other resources such as RAM and Persistent Storage. This post is derived from the notes I made while reading the best OS book on the internet. What is Resource Virtualization?

item.title: Good Morning ...
item.description: Hello there; thank you for stopping by. In this post, I intend to answer the question, “What is this, and why does it exist?” I’m setting up this blog as a space for me to “learn in public”. I intend for it to serve as a quasi-journal into which I distill the different things that I’ll be learning, working on, and thinking about. I expect the focus of the posts to be on two main areas: Programming and product.
